message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): build_tls_server_name contract tests (tick 334)

Added four narrow tests pinning `build_tls_server_name`'s behavior:

- `build_tls_server_name_accepts_ipv4_literal` — `127.0.0.1:8443` produces
  `ServerName::IpAddress` (rustls expects this variant for IP-addressed
  targets; a `ServerName::DnsName` variant would attempt DNS cert
  verification and fail).
- `build_tls_server_name_accepts_dns_hostname` — `example.com:443`
  produces `ServerName::DnsName`.
- `build_tls_server_name_defaults_empty_host_to_localhost` — empty bind
  addr defaults to `"localhost"` rather than empty string or panic
  (pins the explicit default in the code at distributed.rs:872-876).
- `build_tls_server_name_rejects_invalid_host` — `"bad host name:443"`
  (spaces invalid in both DNS names and IP literals) returns
  `DistributedTlsError::Config(_)` rather than accepting a malformed
  name.

Why this matters: `build_tls_server_name` is the SNI / server-name-
verification surface. The name it returns is what the TLS connector
uses to verify the server's certificate. A regression that silently
accepted malformed input (e.g. stripped invalid characters instead
of erroring, or fell back to a DnsName for an IP) would open a
cert-verification hole — TLS would complete but against the wrong
name.

The IPv4 vs DNS variant split is specifically load-bearing: rustls
ServerName::IpAddress verifies IP SANs; ServerName::DnsName verifies
DNS SANs. Mis-routing between them breaks certificate matching.

During development, discovered that `":8443"` (empty host before
colon) does NOT trigger the empty-host default branch — it falls
through to literal passthrough in `distributed_bind_host`, which then
fails ServerName::try_from. The actual default-to-localhost branch
only fires for truly empty or whitespace-only bind addresses. Test
adjusted + comment added.

Test result: 4 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 59.63s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
